title: The Buddha as Exemplar and Archetype
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and structured coverage of the Buddha's life, organized into 12 contemplative stages from his initial resolution through enlightenment to his first teaching. It combines biographical narrative with practical spiritual application, rich personal anecdotes, and maintains excellent literary flow. The talk explicitly frames the Buddha as both exemplar and archetype, making it ideal as a spine for a chapter on 'The Life of the Buddha' to which other material can be grafted.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma presents the Buddha's life journey in 12 stages—from his initial resolution under Dipankara Buddha through renunciation, training, enlightenment, and first teaching—using each phase as a contemplation point for practitioners. The talk weaves biographical narrative with practical spiritual guidance, personal anecdotes, and accessible explanations of key Buddhist concepts.

relevance.note: Buddha's life journey as spiritual development template
